p.strong: Parser → Executor → Fork Boundary → Isolation Model

g.text: Phase 4: What Gets Inherited vs Isolated

div.p: Why _exit() and NOT exit() after fork? exit() calls atexit() handlers and flushes stdio buffers. The child inherited copies of the parent's buffered stdout/stderr. If the child calls exit(), it may flush data that the parent was buffering. Result: double output or corrupted files. _exit() terminates immediately without touching stdio buffers. This is the ONLY safe way to exit from a forked child.

text.tspan: // exit() would flush parent's stdio buffers!
// exit() would flush parent's stdio buffers!

g.text: Child gets COPY — changes don't propagate back
File offsets are SHARED between parent and child
Child can modify freely — parent sees original
